scope_summary: Problem: from X import * currently ignores __all__ in Mamba, binding all public names even when the module defines __all__ to restrict exports. Success Criteria: (1) When __all__ is a list/tuple of string literals at top level, from X import * binds only those names. (2) When __all__ is absent, fall back to current behavior (all public names — no leading underscore). (3) AttributeError raised at import time if a name in __all__ does not exist in the module namespace. Boundary: In scope — runtime/module.rs (mb_import_star function + __all__ preservation in compile_and_exec_module), runtime/symbols.rs (register mb_import_star), lower/hir_to_mir.rs (detect star-import and emit CallExtern to mb_import_star), resolve/pass.rs (skip star symbol definition). Out of scope — __all__ manipulation after import, re-exporting via __all__, IDE hints. No contradictions found between requirements and existing specs. The module.md spec already contains a complete change spec with requirements, scenarios, test plan, and file changes.
